contradiction_text: Write gating alone outperformed the combined write-gating-plus-Self-RAG condition, which cuts against the expectation that adding another filtering layer should help.
related_claim_texts: In distractor-heavy evaluations, write gating outperformed ungated retrieval and usually outperformed the paper's Self-RAG comparison across synthetic scaling, Wikipedia, procedural pharmacology, and 2026 arXiv datasets.
disclosure: The article should keep visible that more read-time filtering did not improve an already-clean store in the synthetic comparison and instead matched the weaker Self-RAG result.

contradiction_text: The paper frames write gating as outperforming Self-RAG, but the reported comparison is not a clean head-to-head against the trained original Self-RAG model.
related_claim_texts: In distractor-heavy evaluations, write gating outperformed ungated retrieval and usually outperformed the paper's Self-RAG comparison across synthetic scaling, Wikipedia, procedural pharmacology, and 2026 arXiv datasets.
disclosure: The article should state plainly that the Self-RAG result is bounded by limited sample sizes and by use of a Self-RAG-style prompted critic rather than the trained Self-RAG model.

contradiction_text: Source reputation is part of the scoring method, but removing the source-label signal caused only a modest accuracy drop in the reported Wikipedia setting.
related_claim_texts: The source-label signal contributed only a modest accuracy gain on Wikipedia at 4:1 distractors, reducing write-gated accuracy from 97.8% ± 1.1% to 96.4% ± 1.4% when removed.
disclosure: The article should keep visible that source labels help, but the reported gain is small in this setting, so they should not be presented as the main driver of performance.
